name: serialize_empty_is_empty
reason: Serializing an empty sequence should produce empty bytes — if fold_left injected phantom data for zero elements, downstream combinators would be corrupted

name: serialize_ignores_count_field
reason: `spec_serialize` uses `fold_left` over `vs` without referencing `self.1` — the count field is completely ignored during serialization

name: wf_helper_equals_wf
reason: `wf` and `wf_helper` should be equivalent when `n == self.1` — if they diverged, the roundtrip proof using `wf_helper` would not transfer to the `wf`-based trait contract

name: serialize_append_splits
code:
proof fn phi_4_serialize_append_splits<C: SecureSpecCombinator>(c: &RepeatN<C>, vs: Seq<C::Type>)
    requires
        vs.len() > 0,
    ensures
        c.spec_serialize(vs) == c.spec_serialize(vs.drop_last()) + c.0.spec_serialize(vs.last()),
{
}
reason: Serialization of a sequence should equal serialization of all-but-last concatenated with serialization of the last element — if fold_left didn't decompose this way, the inductive roundtrip proof structure would be unsound

name: max_length_lemma_individual_bound
code:
proof fn phi_5_max_length_lemma_individual_bound<C: SecureSpecCombinator>(c: &RepeatN<C>, vs: Seq<C::Type>, i: int)
    requires
        c.requires(),
        c.wf(vs),
        c.spec_serialize(vs).len() <= usize::MAX,
        0 <= i < vs.len(),
    ensures
        c.0.spec_serialize(vs[i]).len() <= usize::MAX,
{
    c.lemma_spec_serialize_max_length(vs, c.1);
}
reason: If the total serialization fits in usize, each individual element's serialization must also fit — if any individual exceeded usize::MAX while the total didn't, the addition would have overflowed
